So, because of that, I kept developing an affection deficit. Since I'm an only child, I think that has carried over into my adulthood. Exactly. Let me explain. It’s not that I have an affection deficit just because I didn't receive love, as you just described. That might be true, but it was like that for everyone in our generation. Every man in Korea has an affection deficit. The reason is that parents are all busy, and among the parents of the past... Who tells their child, " I love you, I love you"? You’ve never heard that even once in your life, have you? So, do you think your mom and dad did this because they wanted to go out and work? But if you come out here and say you weren't loved, your mom and dad will probably feel the futility of life. They probably raised you no matter what, to pay for your tuition and manage the rest of your life, and that might be their way of showing love. So, of course, your mom and dad might not express their love often, but if I think you have an affection deficit because of that, that is just you hastily judging yourself. It depends on your mindset. I’m telling you, don't make excuses. Understood. Now, think carefully.
